zh.lifeSummary: Lydia Olive Abbott於1772年10月21日出生在美國新罕布夏州洛金哈姆也。她於1793年8月6日與Stephen Randall在美國緬因州約克貝維其可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1860年3月5日在美國緬因州約克諾斯 貝維其可享年87歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lydia Olive Abbott于1772年10月21日出生在美国新罕布什尔州洛金哈姆也。她于1793年8月6日与Stephen Randall在美国缅因州約克貝維其可结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1860年3月5日在美国缅因州約克諾斯 貝維其可享年87岁。

nameMeanings.name: Abbott
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English abbod, abbot(t), abbat ‘abbot’ (Old English abbod) or Old French abet ‘priest’. Both the Old English and the Old French term are derived from Late Latin abbas (genitive abbatis) ‘priest’, from Greek abbas, from Aramaic aba ‘father’. The nickname was presumably a joking reference to a person's behaviour. In the US, the English name is also sometimes a translation of a cognate or equivalent European name, e.g. Italian Abate , Spanish Abad , or German Abt .

History: George Abbot from Yorkshire, England, settled in Andover, MA, in 1640; he had numerous prominent descendants. George Abbott (probably not the same man) died in Rowley, MA, in 1647. Another early migrant was James Abbott, who came from Somerset, England, to Long Island, NY, in the 17th century.

variants.name: Abate
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Antonio, Carmelo, Carmine, Gaetano, Vito, Angelo, Canio, Rocco, Sal, Aldo, Carlo.

Italian: from abate ‘priest’ (from Late Latin abbas ‘priest’, genitive abbatis). It could be a nickname, perhaps given to someone who behaved in a priest-like manner, an occupational name for the servant of a priest, or it may denote an (illegitimate) son of a priest. Compare Labate .

variants.name: Abbot
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of Abbott . The spelling with one -t is more typically Scottish than English, and in Gaelic-speaking areas of Scotland, this is sometimes an Anglicized form of McNabb .

variants.name: Abbotts
meanings: topographic or occupational name from Middle English (atte) abbotes ‘(at the, of the) abbot's (house or quarters)’, a name for someone who lived or worked at such a place, presumably a servant.
variant of Abbott , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Lydia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Greek origin, meaning ‘woman from Lydia’, an area of Asia Minor. The name is borne in the Bible by a woman of Thyatira who was converted by St Paul and who entertained him in her house (Acts 16:14–15, 40). It has enjoyed steady popularity in the English-speaking world since the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Olive
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a medieval given name, Oliff(e), derived via French from the Late Latin name Oliva, which was borne by two obscure early saints. It in turn is derived from the Latin name for the olive tree, and was no doubt adopted at first because of the associations of the olive tree with peaceful productivity and fruitfulness; the olive branch has been a symbol of peace since biblical times. Like many names denoting plants and flowers, it was particularly popular in the early decades of the 20th century. See also Olivia .

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.

historicEvents.title: Whitehead Light
historicEvents.description: In 1804, President Thomas Jefferson authorized the creation of a light station on Whitehead Island. The light house went into service by 1807. It is the third-oldest light house in Maine. Whitehead Light still exists as the private property of Pine Island Camp, a non-profit organization.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.
Historical Boundaries: 1831: York, Maine, United States

historicEvents.title: Webster–Ashburton Treaty
historicEvents.description: The Webster-Ashburton Treaty was signed on August 9, 1842 and resolved the border issues between the United States and British North American colonies which had caused the Aroostook War. The treaty contained several agreements and concessions. It called for an end on the overseas slave trade and proposed that both parties share the Great Lakes. It also reaffirmed the location of the westward frontier border (near the Rocky Mountains) as well as the border between Lake Superior and Lake of the Woods. The treaty was signed by Daniel Webster (United States Secretary of State) and Alexander Baring (British Diplomat, 1st Baron Ashburton).
historicEvents.urlTitle: Office of the Historian - Webster-Ashburton Treaty, 1842

historicEvents.description: U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

en.lifeSummary: Lydia Olive Abbott was born on 21 October 1772, in Rye, Rockingham, New Hampshire, United States. She married Stephen Randall on 6 August 1793, in Berwick, York, Maine, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 5 March 1860, in North Berwick, York, Maine, United States, at the age of 87.

Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 5 mars 1860, à North Berwick, York, Maine, États-Unis, à l’âge de 87 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Lydia Olive Abbottは1772年10月21日にアメリカ合衆国ニューハンプシャー州ロクインガムリで生まれた。1793年8月6日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークバーウィックでStephen Randallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1860年3月5日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークノース バーウィックで87歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lydia Olive Abbott(은)는 1772년 10월 21일 미국 뉴햄프셔 주 록잉함 리에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1793년 8월 6일 미국 메인 주 요르크 버윅에서 Stephen Randall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1860년 3월 5일 미국 메인 주 요르크 노르스 버윅에서 87세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Lydia Olive Abbott родилась 21 октября 1772 г. в местности ри, роккингхам, Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Stephen Randall 6 августа 1793 г. в местности бирвикк, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 5 марта 1860 г. в местности норт бирвикк, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 87 лет.
